title: Context Performance and Best Practices
description: Avoid unnecessary re-renders and structure context wisely.

slides.title: Performance Considerations ⚡
slides.content:
# Context Re‑renders

When the context value changes, **all** components that consume that context will re‑render, even if they only use part of the value.

## Optimization Strategies

1. **Split contexts** – separate unrelated data into different contexts.
2. **Memoize the context value** – use `useMemo` to avoid unnecessary re‑creations.
3. **Use state selectors** – custom hooks that return only needed parts.

## Example: Splitting Contexts

Instead of one giant context for user + theme + settings, create separate contexts: `UserContext`, `ThemeContext`, `SettingsContext`.

This way, changing the theme won't re‑render components that only consume user data.

slides.title: Memoizing Context Value
slides.content: Prevent value from changing on every render of the provider:

quizData.question: What happens to all consumers when a context value changes?

options.text: React optimizes and only updates changed parts

quizData.explanation: All components that use `useContext` on that context will re-render when the value changes, regardless of which part they use.
